text: Messi scores again but Argentina given World Cup upset fright by Cape Verde - Al Jazeera
fullText: Lionel Messi opens scoring but Argentina taken to extra time by Cape Verde, which threatened greatest World Cup upset.

text: After weeks of speculation, Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wed in New York - NPR
fullText: Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.

text: Family-owned company prepares to put on the largest fireworks display in history: "It is the biggest show that we've ever done" - CBS News
fullText: When the sun goes down on Independence Day, the skies of Washington, D.C., are expected to fill with a record-setting 850,000 individual fireworks for a 40-minute spectacle like no one has seen before.

text: Trump to speak at Mount Rushmore, one monument he can’t remake - NBC News
fullText: No presidential distinction rivals having one’s likeness carved onto Mt. Rushmore — an honor destined to elude Donald Trump, though his allies have pushed for it.

text: Carlsson gets offer sheet from Flyers; Ducks can match - NHL.com
fullText: Contract is for 5 years, $90 million; Anaheim would receive 4 1st-round picks as compensation

text: Thousands forced to evacuate in Colorado as wildfire spreads - Al Jazeera
fullText: The Aspen Acres Fire, one of about 40 wildfires burning across western US, has destroyed homes and forced evacuations.

text: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown after Celtics-Sixers blockbuster trade: 'Forever grateful' - Yahoo Sports
fullText: Tatum and Brown defined Celtics basketball for nine years.

text: Behind the ‘Supergirl’ Bomb: Competing Cuts, Creative Differences (Exclusive) - The Hollywood Reporter
fullText: For months, DC Studios knew the film wasn't working. Things came to a head in March when the studio tested its own cut against one from filmmaker Craig Gillespie.

text: Annual Capitol concert to go forward Friday, despite heat, but gates open later - The Washington Post
fullText: Temperatures on Friday and Saturday are expected to exceed 100 degrees.

text: Who are the ultratraditionalists rebelling against Pope Leo and triggering a rupture with the Vatican? - NBC News
fullText: In a rare move this week the Vatican’s doctrine office declared the Society of St. Pius X in schism and decreed the excommunication of its bishops and priests.

text: Trump pardons 11 people, including several for Clean Air Act violations - CNN
fullText: President Donald Trump pardoned 11 people on Friday, a White House official told CNN, including several individuals who violated the Clean Air Act aimed at cutting down on emissions and improving air quality.

text: The browser wars aren't about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari - TechCrunch
fullText: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

text: Iran begins week of funeral ceremonies for slain Supreme Leader Khamenei - Al Jazeera
fullText: Top Iranian officials and foreign dignitaries pay respects to late leader killed in joint US-Israeli strike in February.

text: Nearly half of Americans surveyed don't know what America 250 commemorates - NPR
fullText: A new national poll reveals a striking paradox in public sentiment ahead of America's 250th anniversary: a disconnect between Americans' strong patriotic pride and their lack of civic knowledge.

text: Ferrari Finally Brought Back The Manual—Sort Of - Motor1.com
fullText: The new Ferrari 12Cilindri Manuale is a special trim with a gearbox that offers both manual and automatic driving modes.

text: Hobbit-like human relatives may have been on a less advanced evolutionary path - CNN
fullText: Instead of hunting big game or wielding fire, the hobbit-like hominin Homo floresiensis likely scavenged leftovers of prey taken down by Komodo dragons.

text: Rescue mission launches to save NASA telescope that’s falling back to Earth - AP News
fullText: A three-armed spacecraft is rushing to the rescue of a NASA telescope that’s in danger of crashing back to Earth. Northrop Grumman launched Katalyst Space Technologies’ Link spacecraft on Friday from the Marshall Islands in the Pacific. The Pegasus rocket bla…
